title: Beef Bourguignon

overview.description: A classic French beef stew slowly braised with red wine, mushrooms, onions and herbs until the meat is meltingly tender. A wonderful weekend project that freezes beautifully.

ingredients: 1kg beef chuck or braising steak, cut into large cubes
method: Season the beef well with salt and pepper.
Heat the oil in a large casserole and brown the beef in batches. Remove and set aside.
Cook the onions and carrots in the same pan until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the flour and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually pour in the red wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Return the beef to the pan and add the beef stock, bay leaves and thyme.
Bring to a gentle simmer, cover and cook in a 160°C oven for about 3 hours, or until the beef is very tender.
About 30 minutes before the end of cooking, melt the butter in a frying pan and brown the mushrooms and shallots.
Stir the mushrooms and shallots into the stew and cook uncovered for the final 30 minutes.
Remove the bay leaves and thyme stems before serving. Adjust the seasoning if required.

learning.did-you-know: Beef Bourguignon began as a peasant dish in Burgundy, where tougher cuts of beef were slowly cooked in the region's famous red wine until tender.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to brown meat properly, build flavour through deglazing and use slow cooking to transform inexpensive cuts into an elegant meal.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the finest examples of slow cooking. It's perfect for a relaxed Saturday, freezes brilliantly and makes excellent leftovers.
chapman-tips: Brown the beef in batches to develop the deepest flavour.
Use a wine you'd happily drink—the flavour carries through into the finished dish.
Homemade beef stock gives the best results if you have some in the freezer.
Like many slow-cooked stews, it's even better the following day.
notes.batch-cooking: Cool completely before freezing in meal-sized portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently on the hob or in the microwave until piping hot. Excellent with fresh mashed potatoes or crusty bread.
